title: Victory Comes Late,
content:
Victory comes late,
    And is held low to freezing lips
    Too rapt with frost
    To take it.
    How sweet it would have tasted,
    Just a drop!
    Was God so economical?
    His table 's spread too high for us
    Unless we dine on tip-toe.
    Crumbs fit such little mouths,
    Cherries suit robins;
    The eagle's golden breakfast
    Strangles them.
    God keeps his oath to sparrows,
    Who of little love
    Know how to starve!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Victory Comes Late,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
